title: Streaming Internet Radio on Your G4
description: How to listen to live radio streams using iTunes 9 and classic Mac OS X. Import .m3u playlists and enjoy.
author: G4 Audio Team
content:
Why Internet Radio on a G4?

The PowerMac G4 is a perfect jukebox for streaming audio. iTunes 9 and earlier have native support for .m3u and .pls streams. Here's how to set it up.

Step 1: Find a Stream

Visit internet-radio.com on a modern machine to find stations. Look for streams ending in .m3u or .pls. Save the playlist file.

Step 2: Transfer to G4

Copy the .m3u file to your G4 via USB, network share, or even floppy if that's your style.

Step 3: Open in iTunes

Double-click the .m3u file. iTunes will open and start streaming. You can add it to your Library for easy access.

Recommended Stations

  • Ambient Sleeping Pill - 64k stream, perfect for coding
  • Radio Paradise - eclectic mix, high quality but G4 handles it
  • SomaFM - Groove Salad, classic

Troubleshooting

If a stream won't load, try lowering the buffer size in iTunes preferences. Some streams require QuickTime 7.
